items.title: HUD PIH Notice 2026-12 sets FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding levels and key deadlines
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-12 implements Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) funding provisions under the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026 (Public Law 119-75), enacted Feb. 3, 2026. The notice lists major CY 2026 appropriations, including $34.557B for HAP renewal funding, $2.836B for administrative fees, and $600.622M for Tenant Protection Vouchers, plus a $400M HAP set-aside with application deadlines including June 26, 2026 (several categories) and Sept. 25, 2026 (HUD-VASH category).

items.title: CMS guidance: limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens begin Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS said a statutory change will limit states’ ability to claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP coverage for individuals who are not U.S. citizens/nationals or who are outside specific noncitizen categories, beginning Oct. 1, 2026. CMS also noted federal matching funds remain available for treatment of emergency medical conditions and referenced implementation guidance in a State Health Official letter tied to section 71109 of the Working Families Tax Cut legislation (Public Law 119-21).

items.title: SSI: 2026 federal payment amounts rise to $994 (individual) and $1,491 (couple) starting January 2026
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 SSI federal monthly maximum payment amounts are $994 for an eligible individual and $1,491 for an eligible couple, effective January 2026. SSA also lists an essential person amount of $498.

items.title: SSDI work incentives: 2026 SGA rises to $1,690/month (non-blind) and $2,830/month (blind); TWP amount is $1,210
items.summary: SSA’s Red Book lists 2026 Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) amounts of $1,690 per month for most disabilities and $2,830 per month for blindness. The same page lists the Trial Work Period monthly earnings amount as $1,210 and the Student Earned Income Exclusion limits as $2,410 per month up to $9,730 per year.

items.title: IRS opened the 2026 tax filing season on Jan. 26; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2 for many early filers
items.summary: The IRS said it opened the 2026 filing season on Jan. 26, 2026 and began accepting 2025 federal income tax returns. The IRS also said it expects most refunds for taxpayers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) to be available by March 2, 2026 for taxpayers using direct deposit (assuming no other issues), and that ‘Where’s My Refund?’ would show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: FAFSA: federal deadline for the 2025–26 school year is June 30, 2026
items.summary: USA.gov states the federal deadline to submit the FAFSA for the 2025–26 school year is June 30, 2026. The same page also lists June 30, 2027 as the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2026–27 school year.

items.title: WIC FY 2026: USDA sets monthly cash-value fruit/vegetable benefit at $26 (children), $48 (pregnant/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA FNS’ WIC Policy Memorandum 2026-2 (dated Dec. 10, 2025) lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/partially breastfeeding participants. The memo states the FY 2026 inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline amounts.

items.title: WIC income guidelines for July 1, 2026–June 30, 2027: 185% FPL thresholds updated
items.summary: USDA published updated WIC income eligibility guidelines for the period July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027, based on 185% of the federal poverty guidelines. For the 48 contiguous states and D.C., the annual 185% threshold is $29,526 for a household of 1 and $61,050 for a household of 4 (with $10,508 added per additional household member).

items.title: SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustments published by USDA (effective Oct. 1, 2025)
items.summary: USDA FNS posted the SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustments (COLA) page dated Feb. 20, 2026, which covers benefit adjustments that take effect each year for the new federal fiscal year. SNAP COLA updates generally take effect on Oct. 1 (start of the fiscal year), though exact benefit amounts vary by household size and location.

items.title: SNAP FY 2026 ABAWD discretionary exemption allocations posted by USDA
items.summary: USDA FNS posted FY 2026 state allocations for discretionary exemptions from the time limit for able-bodied adults without dependents (ABAWDs). These allocations are used by states to exempt a limited number of ABAWDs from the 3-month time limit when they are not meeting SNAP work requirements.

items.title: LIHEAP: HHS released first FY 2026 block grant funding (including $100M IIJA supplemental) on Nov. 28, 2025
items.summary: HHS’ Administration for Children and Families (ACF) Office of Community Services posted that it released FY 2026 LIHEAP funding on Nov. 28, 2025. The release includes both regular block grant funding and $100,000,000 in supplemental LIHEAP funding provided by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Public Law 117-58).
items.source: HHS ACF Office of Community Services
